events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes bend radius violations in data/comm cabling at rough-complete stage. This is a defect - bend radius violations can damage cables, affect signal performance, and violate TIA-568 standards. NEC 800 covers communications circuits.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium (could degrade performance, not immediately dangerous but non-compliant)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 85%
Confidence: high

    "Data/comm cabling installed with bend radius tighter than manufacturer and TIA-568 limits, risking jacket deformation and pair twist disruption",
    "Sharp bends at conduit transitions, J-boxes, or cable trays visible at rough-in",
    "Potential impaired transmission performance and failed certification testing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-pull or re-route affected cable runs to maintain minimum bend radius per TIA-568 and manufacturer specs",
    "Replace any cable with visible jacket kinking or crushing",
    "Use approved bend radius fittings/supports and verify with certification tester before close-in"
  ],
  "rationale": "Bend radius violations at rough-in are a non-compliant installed condition under TIA-568 and NEC 800; they can permanently distort twisted-pair geometry and cause certification failures, so the work must be corrected before closure.",
